entry.title: Re: Current vs Non-Current Deferred Revenue Handling in E...
entry.content:
Hi Shubham101,

To address your requirement directly: in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602, there is no standard, out-of-the-box functionality within pure Event-Based Revenue Recognition (EBRR) to automatically reclassify deferred revenue into current and non-current G/L accounts, nor is there a standard analytical report that provides this split.

Here is the description of how the system handles this, based on the current SAP capabilities:

1. Reclassification to Separate G/L Accounts Pure Event-Based Revenue Recognition (EBRR) is designed for real-time cost and revenue matching. Deferrals and accruals are calculated and posted during real-time events and period-end closing runs. However, these postings are aggregated G/L balances. Because EBRR G/L line items do not natively carry an individual "due date" (unlike AP/AR open items), standard Advanced Valuation processes like Post B/S Reclassification by Maturity cannot evaluate them.

2. Standard Reporting for Current vs Non-Current Split Since the underlying data model of EBRR does not store maturity dates for deferred revenues on Sales Orders, Projects, or Service Documents, there are no standard analytical apps that can filter or split these balances into current and non-current buckets.

My Recommended Approaches: Handling deferred revenue splits manually via spreadsheets is very inconvenient. If a strict current/non-current split is a mandatory reporting requirement, you have two primary paths:

  • Path A: Transition to Contract-Based Revenue Recognition (CBRR / Scope Item 3KK) This is the standard SAP recommendation. CBRR maintains a strict performance obligation (POB) fulfillment schedule. It includes the standard app Contract Balance Reclassification, which automatically analyzes the remaining duration of POBs and generates the necessary journal entries to split the balances into short-term (current) and long-term (non-current) G/L accounts.

  • Path B: Custom Extensibility & API Workaround (If remaining on pure EBRR) If activating 3KK is strictly out of scope, a custom automation approach might be used. You would need to build a Custom Analytical Query exposing EBRR balances combined with the end dates of the underlying objects (e.g., Project end dates). You could then extract this data, process the maturity logic externally and post the reclassification back into the system using the standard Journal Entry API. Please note that you cannot post manual journal entries directly to standard EBRR-managed accounts; your automated postings must use separate adjustment G/L accounts.

Hope it helps,

Best Regards,

Michal

entry.title: Re: Shipment Cost Pricing Destination tariff zone override
entry.content:
Consider to implement the below logic that:

  • Reads all deliveries in the shipment.
  • Determines the tariff zone for each delivery.
  • Compares the zones against the maintained tariffs.
  • Selects the “winning” zone (highest cost).
  • Overrides the zone used in shipment cost calculation before pricing is executed.

The most appropriate enhancement point is BAdI LE_SHIPMENT_COSTS or user exit in function module TMS_CI_PRICE_DETERMINE. Here you can override the tariff zone field before the pricing procedure is called. Keep the logic simple and loop through deliveries, determine zones, call condition record check, pick the highest, and set it.

entry.title: Qualifications Replication Between SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud and SAP Field Service Management
entry.content:
Context

Imagine a customer calls in because a piece of hydraulic equipment just broke down on their factory floor. Your dispatcher needs to send someone out — fast. But not just anyone. They need a technician who actually knows hydraulics. Send the wrong person and the job doesn't get fixed on the first visit, the customer is frustrated, and your team has wasted a trip.

This is the problem that skill matching solves. Your company already knows which technicians hold which qualifications — that information lives in your SAP system. Your field scheduling tool (SAP Field Service Management, or FSM for short) is where dispatchers actually assign jobs. The goal of everything in this guide is simply to connect those two: get the skill information from SAP into FSM so the scheduler can automatically find the right person for every job.

Connecting SAP to FSM

  • Refer setup-for-s-4hana-and-sap-field-service-management for how to maintain FSM system details in S/4 HANA system. 
  • Configure the FSM replication settings in the SAP IMG by navigating to: SAP Customizing Implementation Guide → Service → Integration with Other SAP Solutions → SAP Field Service Management → Configure Integration. This activity sets the RFC destination, Account/Company IDs, Client Version, and the REST API paths used to communicate with the FSM cloud tenant.

     

     

    Single Company Setup

    • For a single-company deployment, provide the FSM Account ID and Company ID in the same IMG node: SAP Customizing Implementation Guide → Service → Integration with Other SAP Solutions → SAP Field Service Management → Configure Integration. These values uniquely identify your FSM tenant and ensure that replicated objects are routed to the correct company context.

       

      Multiple Company Setup

      • When multiple SAP company codes need to replicate data to different FSM companies, define the company-to-company mapping in: SAP Customizing Implementation Guide → Service → Integration with Other SAP Solutions → SAP Field Service Management → Configure Multiple Company Replication. Each entry maps an S/4HANA company code to its corresponding FSM company, allowing fine-grained control over which data is shared with which FSM tenant.

        Scope

        Scope of this document 

        1. Scale and Proficiency replication
        2. Qualification and Qualification Group replication
        3. Assignment of Qualification/Skill to Employee
        4. Assignment to Qualification/Skill to Service Order and Activity 

          Note:- Qualification assignment to Maintenance Orders are not yet supported 

          Scale replication

          • Scale replication is a ‘manual’ activity — unlike Qualification replication, it does not trigger automatically when a Scale is created or changed. You must explicitly execute the replication program described below.
          • Before proceeding, verify that all prerequisite configuration steps have been completed. Refer to the ‘Configure Replication of Scales’ IMG activity for the full checklist (RFC destination, API paths, Account/Company IDs). Configure Replication of Scales

            Step 1: Create Scales in S/4HANA

            • Open the IMG activity ‘Maintain Scales’ by navigating to: SAP Customizing Implementation Guide → Service → Basic Functions → Qualifications → Maintain Scales. This is the starting point for defining all proficiency scales that will later be replicated to FSM as ‘Levels’ within a skill category.

              Fig 5: SPRO path to define Scale

        Fig 4: Multi-company Config settings

    Fig 2: FSM Config parametes

    Fig 1: Maintain FSM Configuration SPRO path

entry.title: Issue with PO Item level conditions during Third-party PO creation
entry.content:
When creating a Third-party Purchase Order in ME21N from a Third -party purchase requisition, conditions irrelevant to the PO type for Third party Purchase order are populated in the PO Item level conditions tab. 

When trying to create a Third-party Purchase Order via ME21N, if we select the PO Type for Normal Purchase order, adopt the Third-party Purchase Requisition, then we change the PO type for Third-party purchase order, the conditions that were populated for Normal PO Type are still listed in the PO Item level conditions tab.

entry.title: Tax Reporting Date
entry.content:
Hi, 

Our customer is running Public Cloud and have requirment to update the tax reporting date for some documents. I can find that this functionality exists for Spain with Schedule Tax Date Updates. Our customer is based in Sweden so we cannot use the same job as for Spain. We found the possibility of changing this date in app Run Statutory Reports with Manage Tax Items For Legal Report. We'll have several document that will need to update this field with so working with Manage Tax Items for Legal Report feels a bit manual. Is there any other way of update this date without doing this manual workaround?

Kind Regards, Sanne

entry.title: Improving SAP Analytics Cloud Story Performance with a Simple Landing Page
entry.content:
Introduction

Performance issues can negatively impact user experience in SAP Analytics Cloud, especially when stories load large volumes of data immediately upon opening.

In one of our reporting stories, users frequently experienced long loading times, system hanging, and occasional crashes. A simple design change significantly improved the user experience.

Issue

The story contained multiple tables and charts that loaded data at the all products level as soon as the story opened.

As a result:

• Large amounts of data were retrieved immediately.

• Story opening time increased significantly.

• Users experienced system hanging and slow response.

• In some cases, the story became unresponsive.

Solution

Instead of opening the story directly on a data-intensive page, I created a blank landing page without any tables, charts, or other data-consuming widgets.

The landing page contains only filter controls and an instruction message for users.

Message Displayed to Users

"Welcome to the story.

This first page is kept intentionally blank to improve performance and avoid heavy data loading.

Please select and update all required filters first.

After setting the filters, go to the next page to view the data.

This helps reduce loading time and avoids system hanging or crashes."

Benefits

The solution provided several benefits:

• Reduced initial data loading.

• Faster story opening time.

• Lower system resource consumption.

• Improved user experience.

• Reduced complaints about hanging and crashes.

• Increased user satisfaction.

Why This Works

When users first apply the required filters, the amount of data retrieved on subsequent pages is significantly reduced.

Instead of loading all available data at story startup, the system loads only the filtered data required by the user.

This simple approach improves performance without modifying BW queries, models, or backend systems.

Conclusion

Performance improvements do not always require complex technical changes. Sometimes a simple design enhancement can make a significant difference.

Creating a lightweight landing page that encourages users to set filters before viewing data helped reduce loading issues and improved overall user satisfaction in SAP Analytics Cloud.

entry.title: Optimizing SAP Fiori Elements for Mobile Users
entry.content:
Published: 09th June 2026 | Reading time: 5 minutes 

In today's digital era, let me ask you a simple question: Which device do you choose while traveling or enjoying coffee in a breakout area—your laptop or your mobile phone? 

Or imagine you're a quality inspector in a manufacturing plant, constantly moving between machines throughout the day. Would you carry a bulky laptop, or would you prefer the convenience of a mobile phone? 

I'm willing to bet you chose mobile. And you're not alone. Mobile phones have become our go-to productivity tools—handy, accessible, and ready wherever we are, whether on a bus, train, or at a coffee table. 

The Mobile Gap We Need to Close 

As mobile devices dominate our work lives, our customers and users naturally expect a seamless experience when using SAP Fiori elements applications. 

Current State: Good, But Not Great 

Today's SAP Fiori elements applications are responsive—they adjust controls based on screen size and device type. That's a great foundation, but it's not the complete picture. 

The Reality Check 

Despite the responsive design, users still face friction: 

  • Multi-dimensional scrolling: Navigating horizontally and vertically between filter fields 
  • Table challenges: Squinting and swiping through rows and columns 
  • Compromised UX: Functional, yes. Delightful? Not quite. 

This gap between "it works" and "it works beautifully" became the catalyst for our innovation. 

SAP Fiori Elements Mobile Phone Optimization Goes Beyond Responsive Design 

Introducing our game-changer: SAP Fiori elements mobile phone optimization—a product designed to deliver the best-in-class mobile experience your users deserve. 

List report page using the SAP Fiori elements mobile phone optimization  

Object page using the SAP Fiori elements mobile phone optimization  

What Makes It Special 

This isn't just about shrinking desktop interfaces. It's about reimagining the entire mobile experience: 

  • Native mobile feel: Controls optimized for touch interactions 
  • Intuitive navigation: No more frustrating scrolling marathons 
  • Smart layouts: Content adapts intelligently to mobile screens 
  • Performance first: Fast, responsive, and fluid 

Addressing Your Concerns 

You might be thinking: "Sounds good, but what about the development effort and learning curve?" 

Good news – we've designed this with you in mind: 

  • Zero additional coding: Works out of the box 
  • Automatic optimization: Smart defaults that just work 
  • Familiar patterns: Built on SAP Fiori elements concepts you already know 

Why Mobile Optimization Matters 

Mobile optimization isn't just a feature—it's a necessity in today's mobile-first world. With SAP Fiori elements mobile phone optimization we're not just meeting expectations, we're setting a new standard for mobile enterprise applications. 

What's Next 

Stay tuned for our upcoming series where we'll dive deep into key features and capabilities. 

Join the Early Customer Care (EAC) Program 

Ready to experience the difference and help share the product? 

Sign up and share your feedback: SAP Customer Influence

Have questions or thoughts? Drop a comment below. 

Siva Rama Krishna Pendyala is a Product Manager on the SAP UX Foundation team. Connect on LinkedIn.

entry.title: SAP BTP Trial – Booster and Subscription Errors with SAP Build Apps
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

Today my SAP BTP trial expired, and I subscribed to a new trial account. While setting up, I ran into several issues related to SAP Build Apps booster and entitlements.

Entitlement Differences Between Old and New Trial

  • In my previous trial (now expired), I could clearly see SAP Build Apps listed under entitlements.

  • In the new trial, when I checked entitlements, SAP Build Apps does not appear at all.

  • This difference is blocking me from running the booster successfully.

     

    Attached screenshots show the current entitlement list (no SAP Build Apps) and the previous trial entitlement list (SAP Build Apps available).

    Booster Execution Error

    • When I tried to run the SAP Build Apps booster, I encountered a “Missing Entitlements” error.

    • The booster checks failed, specifically pointing out that SAP Build Apps (free plan) was missing.

    • This prevents me from completing the booster setup.

       

      Attached screenshot shows the entitlement check failure during booster execution.

      Additionally, SAP Build Apps no longer appears in the Instances and Subscriptions list, making it impossible to create a subscription.

       

      Attached screenshot shows the missing SAP Build Apps option in the subscription creation screen.


      Is there a workaround to enable SAP Build Apps in the new trial?


      How to resolve entitlement issues so the booster can run successfully.

      Any guidance or workarounds from the community to resolve these SAP Build Apps booster and entitlement issues would be greatly appreciated.

      Best Regards,
      Yashmin Behera

       SAP Build Apps 
      SAP BTP cockpit 

      @HristoSimeonov

entry.title: SAP Academic MeetUp @ SAP Ratingen
entry.content:
Liebe Lehrende in Nordrhein-Westfalen,
wir laden Sie herzlich zu einem SAP Academic Meet-Up ein – einem Abend für den Austausch rund um SAP-Themen in Lehre, Forschung und die Zusammenarbeit mit anderen Lehrenden und SAP.

📅 Wann? 2. Juli 2026 | ab 17:00 Uhr
📍 Wo? SAP Geschäftsstelle Ratingen (https://lnkd.in/dR-gkezs)

Was erwartet Sie?
✅ Einblicke in aktuelle SAP- & KI-Themen, u.a. Agentic AI
✅ Vorstellung von Ressourcen für den direkten Einsatz in der Lehre
✅ Praxisbeispiele von akademischen Einrichtungen
✅ Networking mit SAP-ExpertInnen & Lehrenden anderer Hochschulen
✅ Snacks & Getränke inklusive 🍕🥤

Die Veranstaltung richtet sich an Lehrende (ProfessorInnen & Lehrkräfte) die bereits mit SAP Lösungen arbeiten oder Interesse daran haben. Die Teilnahme ist kostenlos, Parkplätze sind vorhanden.

👉 Jetzt kostenlos anmelden über Eventbrite – Plätze sind begrenzt!
https://lnkd.in/dtGQ-US7

Leiten Sie diese Einladung gerne an interessierte KollegInnen in der Region weiter – wir freuen uns über bekannte Gesichter und neue Lehrende gleichermaßen!

entry.title: Re: Peru - Costing - all materials with MAP
entry.content: Please note Standard Price (S) vs. MAP (V) is a universal SAP concept, not country‑specific.  Having said that, there is no Peru‑specific SAP solution for MAP; compliance is achieved by configuring all materials with price control “V” and using Material Ledger.

entry.title: How to Support the Circular Economy with SAP Responsible Design and Production
entry.content:
Why Circular Economy Management Matters

Organizations face increasing pressure to reduce packaging waste, improve recyclability, and comply with evolving environmental regulations. Managing packaging data and extended producer responsibility (EPR) requirements manually can quickly become complex.

SAP Responsible Design and Production help businesses support circular economy strategies by enabling packaging transparency, compliance reporting, and sustainability-driven product design.

With SAP Responsible Design and Production, companies can:

  • Track packaging compositions and recycled material content
  • Support circular business models and sustainable product design
  • Manage packaging taxes and EPR reporting requirements
  • Improve data quality and compliance visibility
  • Integrate sustainability into operational processes

What You’ll Learn in This Learning Journey

This comprehensive, 16-hour learning journey introduces the key concepts and capabilities of SAP Responsible Design and Production:

  1. Introduction to Circular Economy Concepts
    Understand the principles of circular economy and the role technology plays in enabling sustainable business models.
  2. Packaging Data and Compliance
    Learn how to review packaging data, manage packaging compositions, configure design rules, and monitor compliance requirements.
  3. Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) Reporting
    Discover how to generate EPR and NGO reports and understand country- and region-specific reporting requirements.
  4. Administration and Data Management
    Configure report categories, replicate and import data, manage data access, and monitor data quality.
  5. Packaging Tax Reporting
    Learn how to prepare, calculate, and analyse plastics packaging tax reports.
  6. Sustainability Management and Dashboards
    Use reporting dashboards to monitor sustainability performance, issues, and upcoming compliance tasks.

Who Should Take the Course?

This learning journey is ideal for:

  • Administrators
  • Business Users

No prerequisites are required, making it suitable for beginners.

Build the foundation for circular economy compliance and sustainable packaging management and explore the Learning Journey “Administering and Using Responsible Design and Production for the Circular Economy” here.

entry.title: Hello SAP Professionals,I would appreciate your guidance regarding my SAP career path.
entry.content:
Hello SAP Professionals,

I would appreciate your guidance regarding my SAP career path.

I started my career as an SAP Plant Maintenance (PM) end user and later completed SAP PM functional training. After moving into IT consulting, I joined an organization expecting to work in SAP PM Functional Consultant, but was assigned to an SAP MM support role, where I gained around 3 years of experience.

Recently, I moved to another project and have again been assigned to an SAP PM project. However, the project is primarily support-oriented and has limited learning opportunities. As a result, I feel that my experience is spread across both MM and PM, but I have not had the opportunity to gain deep expertise in either module.

Interestingly, I have been able to clear more interviews for SAP PM roles than SAP MM roles, which has made me question which path I should focus on for long-term career growth.

My questions to experienced SAP professionals are:

  1. Should I focus on SAP MM or SAP PM for the next phase of my career?
  2. Which module offers better long-term growth and opportunities?
  3. Given my background in Maintenance Planning, SAP PM, and SAP MM support, which path would help me become functionally stronger?
  4. Would it be beneficial to position myself as a combined MM + PM consultant, or should I specialize in one module?

I feel many consultants get assigned to projects based on organizational needs rather than career goals, making it difficult to build deep expertise in a specific module.

I am looking for guidance from experienced professionals so that I can make an informed decision and focus my efforts in the right direction.

Thank you in advance for your advice and suggestions.

entry.title: Career Crossroads: SAP MM vs SAP PM – Looking for Expert Opinions
entry.content:
Hello SAP Professionals,

I would appreciate your guidance regarding my SAP career path.

I started my career as an SAP Plant Maintenance (PM) end user as Maintenance Planner for Oil and Gas Industry and later completed SAP PM functional training. After moving into IT consulting, I joined an organization expecting to work in SAP PM Functional Consultant, but was assigned to an SAP MM support role, where I gained around 3 years of experience.

Recently, I moved to another Project and have again been assigned to an SAP PM project. However, the project is primarily support-oriented and has limited learning opportunities. As a result, I feel that my experience is spread across both MM and PM, but I have not had the opportunity to gain deep expertise in either module.

Interestingly, I have been able to clear more interviews for SAP PM roles than SAP MM roles, which has made me question which path I should focus on for long-term career growth.

My questions to experienced SAP professionals are:

  1. Should I focus on SAP MM or SAP PM for the next phase of my career?
  2. Which module offers better long-term growth and opportunities?
  3. Given my background in Maintenance Planning, SAP PM, and SAP MM support, which path would help me become functionally stronger?
  4. Would it be beneficial to position myself as a combined MM + PM consultant, or should I specialize in one module?

I feel many consultants get assigned to projects based on organizational needs rather than career goals, making it difficult to build deep expertise in a specific module. I am looking for guidance from experienced professionals so that I can make an informed decision and focus my efforts in the right direction.

Thank you in advance for your advice and suggestions.

entry.title: New POD 2.0 Framework: Implementing Data Scan for Barcode-enabled Component Consumption
entry.content:
This blog post is part of a series on SAP Digital Manufacturing new POD 2.0 framework, the new generation of SAP Digital Manufacturing worker UIs.

New POD 2.0 Framework Blogs

  1. The New Generation of SAP Digital Manufacturing Worker UIs
  2. Template-Based Design Instead of POD Types
  3. Visual WYSIWYG Designer
  4. POD 2.0 Hands-on, Flexible Layout Capabilities, Part 1
  5. POD 2.0 Hands-on, Flexible Layout Capabilities, Part 2
  6. Custom UI Dialogs and Calling Production Processes
  7. Dynamic PODs, How to Dynamically Show, Hide, Enable, and Disable UI Elements
  8. Other Business Capabilities Enabled by POD 2.0
  9. Interactive Value Journey (IVJ)
  10. POD Notifications
  11. Tips and Tricks
  12. Accessing PODs 2.0
  13. Implementing Data Scan for Barcode-enabled Component Consumption

Introduction

The POD 2.0 Data Scan capability enables barcode-driven execution of production activities directly within the POD by configuring barcode parsing and mapping rules. Scanned barcodes can be interpreted using predefined structures and automatically trigger actions such as assembly, data collection, or SFC start, without additional manual steps. This addresses the inefficiency and error risk of manual data entry and navigation during execution.

From a business standpoint, Data Scan reduces operator interaction time, accelerates production steps, and improves data accuracy on the shop floor. It also provides a scalable foundation for automation by reusing configurations across multiple execution scenarios.

In this blog post, I will show you in detail how to implement Data Scan capability to enable Barcode-based Component Consumption.

Prerequisites

Before you begin, make sure that the following prerequisites are met:

  • You have access to SAP Digital Manufacturing and the required authorizations to use the Design Production Processes, Configure Data Scanning, Manage PODs 2.0 apps as well as the Data Scan action. For more information, see Role Templates (Persona-Based).

  • You have a manufacturing scenario master data, from a plant to a production order released with SFCs available to be produced.

Data Fields

First, we need to maintain some Data Fields that will receive traceability information parsed from the Barcode scan during assembly. For this example, in Manage Data Fields app, I have SUPPLIER and SERIAL_NO fields of type Text as follows:

Data Type

Next, we must maintain the Data Fields assignment to a Data Type of type Assembly, which can be done in Manage Data Types app as follows:

Assign Data Type to Component Materials

Finally, the Data Type created must be then assigned as 'Data to Collect at Assembly/Consumption' for the Component Material(s) in Manage Materials app at 'Build' tab as follows:

The values can be collected during the component assembly, for detailed traceability.

Production Process

Next, we need to create the production process that will be triggered by the data scan.
For this example, a very simple production process is required, with the input fields below:

Which should trigger 'Assemble Components' service, using the input fields mapping below:

Save and deploy it.

Data Scan

Creating a data scanning configuration requires a production process that you define as the consumer. You can use the production process assigned to the data scanning configuration for data enrichment, where additional values are fetched using APIs. For example, you can retrieve an SFC based on a scanned serial number. You can also use the production process to trigger POD actions like:

  • Start assembly

  • Collect data

  • Start SFC

  • Refresh POD worklist

To create a data scanning configuration, open 'Configure Data Scanning' app and click 'Create:

Enter a 'Name', 'Description', browse and select the production process you created in the previous at 'Consumer' and keep 'Call Type' as 'Synchronous' as follows:

Then, in 'Consumer Mapping' tab, all the production process input variables will be loaded. For the ones that are coming from the barcode itself, set 'Data Qualifier' as input. For the other ones, which are master or transactional data, you can directly map to the available Data Source inputs available, like Resource, Plant, Operation, SFC etc,...

Finally, in 'Data Qualifier Configuration' tab, click 'Create' to create a new data qualifier.
Once a new data qualifier is created, click to open it, and create the following data qualifiers:

You can learn more about Data Qualifiers at: Data Qualifiers and Encoding Types.

Click 'Apply' and 'Save'.

POD 2.0

Next, we need to link the newly created Data Scan to one of our PODs 2.0. To do that, open Manage PODs 2.0 app and create / load a POD 2.0. In this example, I´m using a Work Center POD Template, so navigate to SFCDetails page, click 'Component Consumption' plugin and add a custom Button element there.

Once the Button is added, click 'Edit Actions' under Events right-hand side Properties panel, to configure the Data Scan call.

Click '+' to add a new Action, select 'Scan Data':

And configure it accordingly by selecting the 'Data Scanning Configurations' previously created:



In my example, I also added the custom Action 'RefreshComponentList' that I created myself, to trigger a Component List refresh automatically right after the Data Scan is completed and the component is assembled, so the assembled component result can be shown right away. 

* RefreshComponentList is a custom development, not available in standard POD 2.0, you will not find it there.
 
Save and Run the POD 2.0 for testing.
 
Run

With all this setup in place, run the POD, enter Work Center and Resource values, and select an existing SFC for processing.
Then, move to 'Component Consumption' plugin, where the custom button we added should show up, and then click it to start the data scan process.


Once you click the custom button, Data Scan plugin will be triggered, which initially allows you to use the device camera to scan the barcode:

Alternatively, you can also click the image icon on the bottom center of this screen, to load the barcode image via file:

Or, you can click the 'X' in the top-left corner to close the device camera preview, which will show you a manual input barcode field, where you can use a barcode scanner to read, or even copy and paste the barcode string value:


Click 'OK' to proceed.

In all three options above, the barcode must follow the data qualifier setup we did, and should look like this:


{CS}MZ_BR_R002{CE}{CVS}ERP001{CVE}{DFNS}SUPPLIER{DFNE}{DFVS}Acme{DFVE}
{CS}MZ_BR_R003{CE}{CVS}ERP001{CVE}{DFNS}SERIAL_NO{DFNE}{DFVS}12345{DFVE}

Where the following parsing and mapping will take place:

{CS}{CE}inComponent
{CVS}{CVE}inComponentVersion
{DFNS}{DFNE}inDataFieldName
{DFVS}{DFVE}inDataFieldValue
 
Where you have not only the component material and version, but also the data field information configured in Manage Materials app at 'Data to Collect at Assembly/Consumption' field, for detailed traceability.
 
You can learn more about Data Qualifiers at: Data Qualifiers and Encoding Types.
 
For demo purposes, I used Online Barcode Generator tool, to generate the barcodes.
 

Finally, as soon as the barcode is scanned, the data scan will run, parse the barcode using the data qualifiers, and map the parsed values to the production process, which will run and perform the assembly. In the POD 2.0, you should see a successfull message like:

And you can manually refresh the POD to see the components assembled.

In SFC Report app, you can open the SFC and check 'Components' tab to see the assembled components, including the assembly traceability data provided for SUPPLIER and SERIAL_NO:

Learn more 

You can learn more about POD 2.0 Data Scan at:

  • Understand Data Scanning Configurations
  • Tutorial: Setting Up Barcode Scanning

Conclusion

The POD 2.0 Data Scan capability provides a powerful and flexible way to streamline shop floor execution by transforming barcode scans into immediate production actions. By combining Data Qualifiers, Production Processes, and POD 2.0 actions, manufacturers can eliminate manual data entry, reduce operator effort, improve traceability, and accelerate component consumption activities.

In this example, we demonstrated how barcode data can be parsed and mapped to production process inputs, enabling automatic component assembly while simultaneously capturing traceability information such as supplier and serial number details. The same approach can be extended to many other execution scenarios, including data collection, SFC processing, inventory transactions, and custom shop floor automation use cases.

As organizations continue their journey toward more connected and automated manufacturing operations, Data Scan serves as a scalable foundation for increasing efficiency, improving data quality, and enhancing the overall operator experience within SAP Digital Manufacturing.

 

Do you like this post? Please let me know in the comments section what you think. Any feedback is highly appreciated.
Or, if you have any questions, please check SAP Community Q&A Area, or comment down below.

Thanks,
Manoel Costa

entry.title: Re: Run Datasphere Task Chains from SAC Multi-Actions via External API Step
entry.content:
Hello,

@albertosimeoni @manu_xu 

I was able to successfully execute a Task Chain with parameters within an SAC multi-action by defining SDS parameters with static values.

For instance, the following JSON body works correctly:

{
  "inputParameters": {
    "P_YEARMONTH_FROM": "202401",
    "P_YEARMONTH_TO": "202412"
  }
}

However, when I created a multi-action with parameters (testing different parameter types) and mapped them to the SDS Task Chain parameters as shown below, I encountered errors:

{
  "inputParameters": {
    "P_YEARMONTH_FROM": "${P_YEARMONTH_FROM}",
    "P_YEARMONTH_TO": "${P_YEARMONTH_TO}"
  }
}

In the logs, the following error appears: “The transformation flow run failed because the input parameter value for parameter ‘P_YEARMONTH_FROM’ is not valid.”

Example payload from the log:

{"P_STATUS":"2C","P_YEARMONTH_FROM":202603,"P_YEARMONTH_TO":202604,"correlationId":"741d8ef2-d48c-40ac-9de5-b0dffc456269","task":"TRANSFORMATION_FLOWS/EXECUTE/DEVELOPMENT/TF_RECAST_SETTING_UPDATE"}

When hardcoding the values in the JSON, they are enclosed in double quotes, which is not the case in the payload above.

Thanks

Regards

entry.title: OBYC Account Determination for GBB and ZOF – Balance Sheet Account versus P&L Account
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have the following issue: In OBYC, for transaction/event key GBB and general modification ZOF, a balance sheet account is assigned for one valuation class (ROH). For all other transactions and modifications, including GBB + ZOF with other valuation classes (HALB, FERT), P&L accounts are assigned instead.

I did not set up this customizing myself, and unfortunately there is nobody left whom I could ask about the original intention.

As a result, the balance sheet account keeps accumulating balances continuously. Is there any possible reason why this could have been configured this way? Otherwise, I would change it to a P&L account and correct the postings that have already been made.

Thank you very much for any support and best regards,

Herbert

entry.title: Join our Espresso Session: Latest Innovations and Autonomous Asset Management for Utilities
entry.content:
Join us for a focused 30-minute Espresso Session for utilities and asset-intensive organizations on June 23 at 4pm. CET. We will show how SAP Intelligent Asset Management is evolving with AI and automation. Discover how integrated asset management, AI-powered decision support, and Joule agents help utilities improve reliability, optimize resources, and achieve operational excellence.

Key Topics:

  • Predictive Maintenance: Learn how AI can forecast potential issues, spot risks early, and help prioritize maintenance to extend the life of equipment and reduce costs.
  • Optimized Field Service: Discover how AI and Joule help assign the right teams, plan the best routes, and support technicians to get work done faster and with fewer mistakes.
  • Actionable Insights and agentic workflows: Explore how data from all operations can be used to make better decisions, continuously improve processes, and align teams for better outcomes.

Who Should Join?

This session is perfect for maintenance managers, field service leaders, operations teams, and anyone responsible for maintenance or service delivery who want to use AI and intelligent tools to improve efficiency, reduce downtime, and enhance performance.

Register here

entry.title: Навчальні пропозиції SAP – Червень 2026 | Розклад вебінарів
entry.content:
Шановні учасники спільноти SAP Community Kyiv,

Раді поділитися насиченим розкладом навчальних сесій SAP на червень 2026 року!☀️ Обирайте теми, які вас надихають, реєструйтесь заздалегідь та додавайте до календаря — червень пройде з користю! 

Час вказано за Києвом | Мова вебінарів — англійська.

Хмарна трансформація та міграція

  • 16 червня, 15:00 – 16:00
    Accelerate Cloud Migrations with Migration Assistants and SAP Cloud ALM
    Дізнайтеся, як SAP переосмислює хмарну трансформацію, розвиваючи SAP Cloud ALM до рівня Платформи виконання трансформацій. Завдяки вдосконаленому інструментарію на основі агентів клієнти можуть прискорити налаштування, підготовку даних, міграцію та автоматизоване тестування, використовуючи потужність Агентного ШІ.

AI та дані

  • 9 червня, 18:00 – 19:00
    SAP Enterprise Support Value Maps – Data & AI Content Updates
    Приєднуйтесь до живої сесії, щоб дізнатися про останні оновлення SAP Enterprise Support, зокрема:
    • SAP Datasphere у Business Data Cloud: розгортання CQC та оптимізація рішень
    • SAP HANA Cloud: серія майстерень з впровадження за участю експертів
    • SAP Business AI & Joule: серія майстерень з впровадження за участю експертів
  • 11 червня, 17:00 – 18:00
    From AI Subscription to AI Consumption: The New Commercial Model After Sapphire
    Після оголошень на Sapphire SAP поступово переходить від традиційної ліцензійної моделі на основі користувачів до гнучкої моделі споживання на основі агентів. Значна частина вбудованих можливостей переходить у категорію Free AI, тоді як нові розширені агенти та асистенти займають їх місце. Цей перехід формує нову комерційну парадигму, орієнтовану на масштабованість, прозорість використання ШІ та тісніший зв’язок між бізнес-цінністю ШІ та операційними моделями підприємств.
  • 25 червня, 16:00 – 17:00
    SAP Runs SAP BDC – Unleashing Human & Artificial Intelligence with Data Products
    Дізнайтеся, як SAP розкриває потенціал людського та штучного інтелекту, використовуючи продукти даних у SAP Business Data Cloud. Ви побачите, як SAP запускає маховик ШІ, даних та застосунків зсередини, та як перетворює SAP BDC на реальні рішення для всієї компанії. На реальних прикладах ви дізнаєтесь, як операціоналізувати продукти даних для прискорення аналітики, розширення можливостей команд та впровадження інновацій на основі ШІ у масштабі.
  • 30 червня, 16:00 – 17:00
    5 Lessons from SAP’s Own Transformation to an Agentic AI Enterprise
    Операційна модель Enterprise AI від SAP забезпечує чіткість та узгодженість у середовищі агентного ШІ. Ви отримаєте уявлення про Golden Path агентного ШІ від SAP — комплексну структуру для побудови, розгортання та управління корпоративними ШІ-агентами на SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) у масштабі.

AI та операції

  • 18 червня, 16:00 – 17:00
    From AI-Driven Insights to Automated Operations with SAP Automation Pilot and SAP Cloud ALM
    Дізнайтеся, як SAP Automation Pilot інтегрується з SAP AI Core для аналізу операційних даних та перетворення аналітики на дії. Сесія демонструє, як різні типи метрик та дані спостережуваності збираються SAP Automation Pilot, аналізуються SAP AI Core та передаються до SAP Cloud ALM для розширеної візуалізації та сповіщень. Цей підхід забезпечує справжню модель операцій із замкненим циклом, де проблеми виявляються, аналізуються та автоматично усуваються.

Архітектура та безпека

  • 10 червня, 17:00 – 18:00
    The Trust Triad: A Playbook for Architecting Secure and Sovereign SAP Landscapes
    Архітектура, безпека та суверенітет — це більше не три окремі розмови. Доктор Міхаель Блашке представляє «Тріаду довіри» — практичний посібник, який об’єднує ці дисципліни на етапі проектування, щоб безпека та суверенітет були закладені з самого початку, а не додавалися постфактум.
    Що ви дізнаєтесь:
    • Тріада довіри: конвергенція архітектури, безпеки та суверенітету
    • Модель спільної відповідальності SAP та її вплив на проектування
    • Практичні підходи до інтеграції безпеки та суверенітету в архітектуру
    • Адаптація SAP Enterprise Architecture Framework для суверенних середовищ
    • Реальні приклади з військового та державного секторів
      Для кого: корпоративні архітектори, офіцери безпеки, власники хмарних стратегій та керівники SAP-програм.
      ❓ Останні 10 хвилин — жива сесія Q&A. Готуйте питання!

Комерція

  • 17 червня, 12:00 – 13:00
    Agentic B2B Commerce: Finding the Fast Track to Profitable Growth
    SAP представляє новий сервіс хостингу JavaScript наступного покоління, розроблений для забезпечення роботи високопродуктивних хмарних вітрин у SAP Commerce Cloud. Сервіс надається через SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) та пропонує гнучкість, масштабованість і зручні інструменти для розробників для сучасних headless та composable commerce рішень.
  • 18 червня, 17:00 – 18:00
    Decoupled JS Hosting for Commerce Storefronts
    SAP представляє новий сервіс хостингу JavaScript наступного покоління для високопродуктивних хмарних вітрин на SAP Commerce Cloud. Через SAP BTP новий сервіс пропонує гнучкість, масштабованість та зручні інструменти для розробників для сучасних headless та composable commerce рішень.

HR та досвід співробітників

  • 21 червня, 17:00 – 18:00
    AI, Data, and Experience: The New Blueprint for HR Service Delivery
    Сучасні співробітники очікують від HR-підтримки безперебійності, персоналізації та миттєвої реакції. Дізнайтеся, як SAP SuccessFactors Enterprise Service Management забезпечує перехід від реактивних моделей обслуговування до проактивної підтримки на основі ШІ, що інтегрує самообслуговування, автоматизацію, аналітику звернень та контекстну аналітику. За результатами сесії ви отримаєте чітку структуру для побудови інтелектуальної HR-сервісної моделі, орієнтованої на досвід співробітників.

Навігація та інструменти SAP

  • 23 червня, 16:00 – 17:00
    New in SAP for Me: Your Intelligent Way to Navigate SAP
    Дізнайтеся, як «Інтелектуальні вхідні двері» SAP for Me надають єдину точку входу на основі ШІ для швидкого пошуку відповідей, підтримки та послуг. Платформа поєднує рекомендації ШІ з центром завдань, роблячи взаємодію швидшою, простішою та ефективнішою.

Інтеграція

  • 30 червня, 17:00 – 18:00
    SAP Integration Suite – Monthly Update June
    SAP Integration Suite — це iPaaS платформа для проектування, розробки, виконання та управління інтеграційними потоками, API та бізнес-подіями, що з’єднують хмарні та локальні процеси, застосунки і дані.
    У програмі: Product Updates та інтерактивна сесія з експертом — Integration Black Belt або #AskAnythingIntegration, де панель експертів відповість на ваші запитання.

Якщо побачили щось цікаве — переходьте за посиланнями у назвах вебінарів, реєструйтесь, поки є місця, запрошуйте колег та діліться знаннями. Всі сесії проходять онлайн, більшість буде доступна у записі🎥

entry.title: Re: WorkforcePerson Integration from SF to S4 HANA via MDI
entry.content:
Good day @KKhadhar 

Thank you for your question.

Yes, your current approach is the correct supported one in Public Cloud. The Public Cloud does not offer a standard Position field in the local Workforce object that is automatically read and stored from MDI as a standard field. Workforce extensibility is provided for this case by creating a custom field in Manage Workforce and mapping the incoming MDI field through the BAdI WFD_MDI_EXT_FIELD_MAPPING.

See Extensibility in Manage Workforce App | SAP Help Portal for more insights.

In simple terms:

  • SuccessFactors and MDI may contain Position, but Public Cloud does not provide a standard equivalent workforce field for it.
  • If the field is not part of the delivered standard worker model in S4H Cloud, you cannot consume it as a standard field.
  • The supported solution is for you to create a custom field and map the MDI value with WFD_MDI_FIELD_MAPPING.
  • If your BAdI mapping is already working, then you are following the expected extensibility approach.
  • A standard field-based replication would only be possible if Position was delivered as a standard supported field in S4H Cloud workforce object in a future release.

I recommend the following:

  1. Keep the custom field in Manage Workforce for Position.
  2. Continue using WFD_MDI_EXT_FIELD_MAPPING to map the MDI Position value.
  3. Check the MDI workforce field mapping to confirm whether Postion is available in MDI but not mapped to a standard S4H Cloud workforce field.
  4. If business requires this as a delivered standard field, then consider raising an SAP influence request.

Here are references for more guidance

Field Mapping Between Employee Central and SAP Master Data Integration: Workforce Person | SAP Help Portal

Influence Opportunity Homepage - Customer Influence

 

Here is a quick system check guide:

Open Custom fields, check that the Position custom field is created for the Workforce business context.                         

In Custom Logic, check implementation of WFD_MDI_EXT_FIELD_MAPPING, replicate one employee from SuccessFactors through MDI.                 

Then open Manage Workforce and confirm that the custom Position field is correctly populated.

 

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Margin Analysis Allocation in SAP S/4HANA – How to Display Percentage Shares per Receiver?
entry.content:
Hi Community,

I am currently working with the Margin Analysis Allocation in SAP S/4HANA (Fiori) and I am running into a transparency issue I hope someone can help me with.

Scenario: We allocate costs from sender objects (e.g. cost centers) to receiver objects (e.g. profit centers) using actual revenues as the allocation base. The logic works as expected: the higher the revenue of a profit center, the higher its share of the allocated costs. For example, if three profit centers have revenues of 600,000 €, 300,000 €, and 100,000 €, the costs are distributed at 60 %, 30 %, and 10 % respectively.

What works: The Fiori app Allocation Results clearly shows the credits on the senders and the debits on the receivers, including the amounts per sender-receiver relationship and the executed allocation cycle/segments. So the what (which amounts were posted) is fully transparent.

My problem: There seems to be no standard report showing the percentage share each receiver obtained from the allocation. In practice, I would like to answer questions such as:

  • What percentage of the total allocation did a specific profit center receive?
  • On which revenue base (tracing factor) was this share calculated?
  • How can I make the distribution transparent to profit center managers or auditors without reverse-engineering the percentages from the posted amounts?

My questions to the community:

  1. Is there a standard Fiori app or report that displays the percentage shares / tracing factors per receiver after an allocation run?
  2. If not, what is your best practice to close this gap – custom CDS views, analytical queries, or direct reporting on the allocation tables?
  3. Are there any SAP Notes or roadmap items addressing this transparency topic for Margin Analysis Allocation?

Any input, experience, or pointers would be highly appreciated.

Thanks in advance and best regards!

entry.title: Re: Managing Multiple Employments in SAP SuccessFactors Learning: Person-Centric Experience
entry.content:
@Romina65 thank you for your question.

I will try to answer in general and if this is not clear please DM me the name of your company and LMS URL where you are thinking to turn on PCX to take further look at your instances.

We are asking customers to use an extra system to not disrupt the day to day activities.

From your message, it sounds that you have the standard trio of instances (production, development, test). If you were to convert test, then you would have to conduct your release testing in the PCX environment - which is different than your production system and this isn't ideal.
Development (or test@prod) can be a candidate to migrate to PCX as well now that the 1H2026 production release has been delivered. So you could use dev instead and keep production and preview aligned.

Finally, regarding a "rollback option", you are correct a system refresh can be used. What does this mean? Say for example you decide to migrate your development tenant to PCX, but after testing it out you realise this doesn't work for you at this time and you prefer to wait and not use PCX. Then you could run a system refresh using your production (non PCX) as the source system for the refresh. Since PCX is not enabled there, it will overwrite the PCX migration on your development instance (the target system of the refresh) thus reverting your Dev instance to it's previous non-PCX state.

entry.title: Is material type INTR (Intramaterial) still officially supported in S/4HANA 2023/2025?
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I've been researching the material type INTR (Intramaterial), which was originally designed for the Process Industry (PP-PI) to represent in-process intermediate materials within a Master Recipe / Process Order.

After extensive research, I have the following findings and open questions:

FINDINGS: -  There is no possibility to create independent Inspection Lots for INTR , unless its manual

 - Quality control for INTR is handled indirectly via Inspection Type 03 (in-process inspection) on the HALB/FERT header material of the Process Order, with MICs assigned at the phase level in the Master Recipe . However, inspection results are recorded against the ORDER/OPERATION, not against the INTR material itself , meaning there is NO direct QM traceability at material level for INTR

- There is no Simplification List item (S4TWL) exists mentioning INTR as deprecated or obsolete in any S/4HANA release

 OPEN QUESTIONS:

1. Is material type INTR still officially supported in S/4HANA 2023 / 2025 On-Premise and Private Cloud?

2. Has SAP ever published any official statement, SAP Note, or roadmap item indicating that INTR is deprecated, to-be-deprecated, or discouraged?

3. Whats the benefit of Intramaterials ? It can be replaced in all cases by HALB.

4. Are there any active S/4HANA customers or industries (pharma, chemical, food & beverage) still using INTR in production systems?

Any guidance from SAP product management, consultants, or experienced practitioners would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: Managing Multiple Employments in SAP SuccessFactors Learning: Person-Centric Experience
entry.content:
@sanchobe thank you for your question.

Could you please share more details about the "SAP services" used for user data sync?

Otherwise, if you look at the Person Connector workbook (zip file with all PCX related documents linked in the main blog post) you will see that we require the mapping of the following EC fields: person_id_external (a user facing customisable ID), userid (the employment id), Person_guid (system unique person ID). These fields are already supported in the SF User Connector today, the difference in the Person Connector is how we process the data (person vs employment data) so you should already be able to sync them. In the event where you have issues in the current framework please share (here or DM) more details about the use case and current process.

Note that the Person ID is an EC specific identifier and this is not used in LMS so it isn't mapped to sync via the connector.

entry.title: Re: Alternative Standard Mobile Application for Batch-Man...
entry.content:
Good day @mertatay 

Thank you for your question.

The error you described most likely means that the Pick Outbound Delivery app does not support maintaining deliveries with batch split or batch managed items. There is no configuration switch to make Pick Outbound Delivery support batch managed delivery items. This is a standard product limitation of the app. For batch managed outbound delivery processing, use Manage Outbound Deliveries Instead.

If you required barcode scanning on handheld devices without EWM, you would usually need a custom mobile app or side-by-side extension using released APIs.

The recommended standard process is as follows:

  1. Use Manage Outbound Deliveries,                                                                                           
  2. search the outbound delivery, go to item details,
  3. maintain or confirm the batch, enter the picked quantity,
  4. save the delivery and post Goods Issue.

For batch determination, ensure that batch management and batch determination are correctly maintained.

See Batches in Delivery Management | SAP Help Portal for more insights.

Check KBA 3699741 if PGA fails with missing batch.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Custom App Interface Publishing Issues of ABAP Development Tools platform
entry.content:
HI,

In the SAP S/4HANA Cloud environment, developers use the ADT (ABAP Development Tools)platform to create custom application tables and publish them as OData interfaces for externalsystem calls. At the same time, certain fields (such as sapdatakey3 ) need to be controlled as read-only on the frontend Fiori UI. The following issues were encountered during implementation:

  1. When the interface is called, data is only written to the draft table, not the base table.
  2. Attempts to use field (features : instance) in the behavior definition to dynamically controlread/write operations fail because HTTP headers cannot be reliably obtained to determine thecaller source
  3. Using field (features : instance) in the behavior definition to control field read-only with%field-sapdatakey3 = if abap behv=>fc-f-read only results in the interface being unable to writedata.
  4. After disabling draft mode, the "Create" and "Edit" buttons disappear from the frontend Fioriapplication.

entry.title: BO 4.3 SP05 upgrade failing because of SQL Anywhere logs
entry.content:
Hello guys
I'm having big problems upgrading an SAP Business Objects Enterprise 4.3 system from SP04 patch 8 hotfix 1 to SP05 patch 5.
I have already upgraded the quality system of the same landscape, but I can't do it in production.
It’s installed on a Virtual Machine running Windows Server 2022, using the SQL Anywhere database that comes bundled.
The system is stand-alone, a single node, and the database is on the same VM on which the SIA and Tomcat also run.
The version of SQL Anywhere is 17.11.7458.
In SQL Anywhere, there are only two databases, the CMS database and the audit database.

So far, I have tried THREE TIMES to upgrade it, but, it has always ended with error, and the behavior has always been the same:
after the deployment of the webapps, the upgrade procedure tries to start the SIA but fails.
In the "setupengine.log" file I see 119 attempts to start the SIA that are unsuccessful, for about 20 minutes, after which the procedure continues but, in the end, reports “ends with errors”.
At the same time as these attempts to start to the SIA, there are numerous error entries in the Windows event viewer. Approximately every 15 seconds:


SQLANYs_SQLAnywhereForBI: Cannot open transaction log file -- Can't use log file "<PATH>\BI4_Audit.log" since it has been used more recently than the database file
SQLANYs_SQLAnywhereForBI: Cannot open transaction log file -- Can't use log file "<PATH>\BI4_CMS.log" since it has been used more recently than the database file

At the end of the activity, the SIA does not even starts.
Regarding the preparation of the activity, I can tell you that I have carried out all the steps reported in the two SAP notes 1952120 and 1757132.
After the first attempt, I realized that, indeed, the two transactional log files were quite large. So, I repeated a second time after making an image backup with truncate logs, but the upgrade went exactly the same as the first time.
For the third attempt I tried to install the VC++ redist 2013 libraries since there was only v14 on the machine, but even this didn't change anything.
So, the upgrade procedure performs some operation that corrupts the database by ending the activity with errors and leaving the system unusable.
What kind of checks can I do to try to figure out what's wrong before trying again for the fourth time?

Please help me!!! 

Thank you all guys.

Andrea

entry.title: Re: Error message KI235 in MIGO in a canceliation document
entry.content:
So you have to check/compare the settings of the 2 movement types (the original and the reversal movement type)

Perform the following checks:

  • Field Status for Additional Account Assignment

    • Compare the field status of the "Additional Account Assignment" in the customizing transactions: OMBW (for movement types)  OMCJ (for special stock)
    • Ensure the field status of the reversal movement type (e.g., 552) matches that of the original movement type (e.g., 551).

  • Account Assignment Check in OMWN

    • Review the customizing transaction OMWN for the reversal movement type.
    • Verify that the "Check Account Assignment" field (T156X-XPKON) is configured identically to the original movement type (if unset for resersal type, can trigger clearing of assignments)

  • Price Difference Line Handling

    • If a price difference line is generated, use OKB9 to define an automatic account assignment for the price difference account.

entry.title: Re: QC21 without usage decision
entry.content:
In standard SAP Quality Management, QC21 (Quality Certificate for Inspection Lot) is designed to work only after a Usage Decision (UD) has been made. The logic is that the certificate reflects the final accepted/rejected status of the lot, so SAP requires the UD as a prerequisite.
Having said that, no configuration exists to make QC21 work without a Usage Decision. If you need certificates earlier, you must either enter a preliminary UD, build a custom certificate program, or use COA functionality.

entry.title: Re: Supplier Confirmation Dunning is not being sent
entry.content:
Hi,

Even if all configuration conditions are met, the reminder is generated only when the purchase order fulfills all prerequisite conditions at the time the background job runs. This includes confirmation control settings, monitoring period, supplier acknowledgement status, and overall PO eligibility during job execution.

If any of these conditions change between PO creation and job execution (for example, supplier confirmation is received, the PO is changed, or the monitoring period has not yet been reached at runtime), the PO will be skipped for that particular run. This can lead to the perceived intermittent behavior where some purchase orders receive reminders while others do not.

KBA https://me.sap.com/notes/0003754711 explains possible reasons. Please refer to the same.

Thanks and regards,

SAP Support

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 03 - Declarative field control with @mandatory
Re: Issue with PO Item level conditions during Third-part...
entry.content:
Why this happens is because, Condition determination is tied to document type + item category at the time of adoption. Changing the PO type after conditions are already determined does not trigger a fresh pricing procedure determination. SAP assumes that if you change the PO type, you will manually re‑determine pricing if needed.

The standard way is to use the Update Conditions function, or you can automate it with a small enhancement in ME_PROCESS_PO_CUST.
